headline: Haiti reached the World Cup without a single home game
summary: Haiti ended a 52-year World Cup absence (first since 1974) by topping their CONCACAF qualifying group ahead of Honduras — while playing ZERO home matches, because ongoing conflict at home forced every 'home' fixture to be staged in Curacao. Striker Duckens Nazon led all CONCACAF qualifiers with 6 goals. They opened WC 2026 with a narrow 0-1 loss to Scotland on June 13, but the journey there is the story.
angles: They had no home — and qualified anyway (the no-home-game stat IS the story)
52 years of waiting ended for a nation that couldn't even play at home
Curacao became Haiti's adopted home ground through the entire qualifying run
Duckens Nazon dragged them there: most goals of any CONCACAF qualifier (6)
Most inspiring story of this World Cup? — debate bait
